thesis: AI observability platform with massive enterprise adoption. Q1 2026 revenue $1.006B (+32% YoY), EPS $0.60 beat. FedRAMP High certification opens federal market. GPU Monitoring GA for AI workloads. ARR exceeded $4B. Analyst PTs: Guggenheim $175 (Buy upgrade), Citi $175 (90-day upside catalyst watch). AI infrastructure tailwind — enterprises need observability as they scale AI workloads. Hold for 3-6 months.

Rezdiffra is the first-and-only FDA-approved treatment for MASH (metabolic-associated steatohepatitis), giving it a virtual monopoly in a disease with no prior standard of care. Q1 2026 net sales $311M (+127% YoY), blockbuster status ($1.1B trailing 12M). 42,250+ active patients. Clear Street PT $680 (from $533). Average analyst PT $671 = ~33% upside from current $505. Company investing in broad pipeline. Thesis: MASH market is large and underpenetrated; Rezdiffra has first-mover advantage. Hold 6-12 months.

Structural oil price tailwind from Iran war / Strait of Hormuz closure ongoing since Feb 28. Brent crude surged ~80% to $109-110/barrel, well above $60-70 industry forecasts. OXY Q1 EPS $1.06 vs $0.60-0.64 consensus — massive beat. Full-year guidance 1,410-1,460 Mboed. CEO Vicki Hollub retiring June 1 (managed transition, stays on board). Berkshire Hathaway continues to back company. Raymond James PT $75 (Outperform). UAE left OPEC May 1 — structural shift benefiting US upstream. Hold 3-9 months.

Visa is the global payments duopoly with a near-indestructible network moat. Fiscal Q2 2026 results strong: EPS $3.31 vs $3.10 expected (+20% YoY), revenue $11.2B vs $10.75B. Transactions +9% YoY, cross-border volume +12% YoY, Visa Direct +23% YoY. Truist raised PT to $371 (from $361). ValueAct Capital increased stake to 2.316M shares (institutional endorsement). Risk: Berkshire sold V position (monitor); rising bond yields may pressure valuation. Hold 6-12 months.

Walmart is a defensive consumer staple that benefits from the consumer squeeze environment (high gas prices, inflation). Near-20% YTD rally, approaching $1T market cap. AI tools, delivery investments, digital ecosystem expanding. Q1 earnings scheduled May 21 — analysts project strong results. Bank of America, Piper Sandler, Telsey avg PT $140 (Strong Buy). Consumer squeeze drives trade-down behavior to Walmart. Risk: double-top technical pattern forming; valuation stretched; tariff impact on goods. Hold through May 21 earnings, reassess.
